description: Cathy Sheeter and I were standing on road that runs along Union Reservoir's n. side. We noticed a lone, rather dark breasted white-cheeked goose. Studying it from some distance, based on white neck collar and extensive black gular patch, plus bronzy breast, I suspected the bird was an Aleutian Cackling Goose. We drove to the closest point, putting us 100 to 150 yards from the bird with fair light.  The birds dark bronzy breast, seen face on (which makes breast look palest, or "true color") was quite dark, as were the remainder of the underparts (obviously, when viewed from side), excepting white undertail coverts and vent. The underpart color was not as dark as most minima, but richer and more bronzy-brown than darkest Tavs. The bird had a typical black neck and head of white-cheeked geese. The cheek patch was very narrow, and without indentation throughout observation. The gular patch was broad, so broad that it could be seen in strict profile views as it came up onto cheek. The bill was relatively long and narrow, the forehead fairly steep, but the bill to forehead angle was not as sharp as typical for Richardson's Cackler. Again, this head shape held true throughout observation, as apparent Cackling Goose head shape can vary from moment to moment depending on activity and alertness. The crown itself was rounded, not angular or flattened. The wing coverts were medium brown for a Cackling Goose, but with bright pale buff edging. No blue or gray hues present. The flanks were moderately barred with diffuse dusky markings, as is often seen in immatures. A thin white collar at base of black neck encompassed about 2/3 of the neck circumference, not easily seen in attached photos. This white collar was subtended by a dark brown band, darker than that of chest below. The bird had a rather chesty appearance that is typical of Aleut and Taverner's, but not Richardson's or minima Cackling Geese. No other geese were present for size comparison, but shovelers and Mallards passed by from time to time, and size was c/w Cackling, not Canada Goose.Behaviors: Standing, then walking, without apparent aim, along muddy edge of Union ReservoirCall: not heardPlumage: immature
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
